task_completion.description: Checks if the agent logged into the 2N Access Commander web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser open to https://localhost:9443 showing the 2N Access Commander dashboard or user management interface.

task_completion.description: Checks if the agent identified Fatima Al-Rashid, revoked her card, and disabled her account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Agent viewing Fatima Al-Rashid's profile (associated with card 0004521879), removing or deleting the RFID card, and toggling her account status to disabled.

task_completion.description: Checks if the agent identified Diana Flores, revoked her card, and disabled her account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Agent viewing Diana Flores's profile (associated with card 0004521884), removing or deleting the RFID card, and toggling her account status to disabled.

task_completion.description: Checks if the agent identified Leon Fischer, revoked his card, and disabled his account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Agent viewing Leon Fischer's profile (associated with card 0007654321), removing or deleting the RFID card, and toggling his account status to disabled.

task_completion.description: Checks that the affected users were disabled but NOT deleted from the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user list still shows Fatima Al-Rashid, Diana Flores, and Leon Fischer, typically with a visual indicator that they are disabled (e.g., greyed out or a disabled badge), rather than being completely removed from the directory.

task_completion.description: Checks if the incident report was created with the correct information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the creation of 'compromised_users_report.txt' in the Documents folder, containing the names and emails: Fatima Al-Rashid (f.alrashid@buildingtech.com), Diana Flores (d.flores@buildingtech.com), and Leon Fischer (l.fischer@secureguard.net).

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used the web GUI to find the users and modify their accounts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the 2N Access Commander web interface in a browser, rather than bypassing the UI using curl or API scripts in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually looked up the users by their card numbers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the search function or browses the user list to match the provided RFID cards to the specific users, rather than magically knowing the names without viewing them in the application.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually created and saved the report file to the correct path.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, gedit) is used to save the file to ~/Documents/compromised_users_report.txt, or terminal commands (like echo/cat) are used to write to this specific path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target users mapped to compromised cards: 0004521879 -> Fatima Al-Rashid (f.alrashid@buildingtech.com), 0004521884 -> Diana Flores (d.flores@buildingtech.com), 0007654321 -> Leon Fischer (l.fischer@secureguard.net). The final report must be located at /home/ga/Documents/compromised_users_report.txt and contain these exact names and emails.
